body_txt: A Long Bus Trip is when a character is Put on a Bus in such a way that arranging a return would be simple, and it even feels like they will be Commuting on a Bus , but then the character never actually comes back. Basically a character version of an Aborted Arc . This is most striking when it's been a decade and the audience starts realizing they never returned, there may even be references to the fact they still exist or in communication with someone in the cast . It may also occur as the result of a series being Left Hanging . There is always the possibility of the character returning, but it turns the situation from a Recurring Character to a Role Reprise . Actor Dies, Character Leaves is a form of this due to circumstances in Real Life , where the Character Outlives Actor . If Died During Production instead results in Back for the Dead or a Bus Crash , that's The Character Died with Him . When this is done to a character in-story as a coverup for execution, it's Released to Elsewhere . Compare Chuck Cunningham Syndrome and Put on a Bus to Hell . Contrast Bus Crash (which kills the character outright) and Back for the Dead (when the character returns briefly, only to be killed off), and The Bus Came Back for that one single return.
